element_introduction_order.element: Basic ice hockey mechanics (skating, passing, shooting)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Practiced in demo, exhibition, and single game modes before playoff pressure

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from game start (new to NHL '94)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Advanced offensive technique requiring practice across multiple games

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from game start (new to NHL '94)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Defensive mechanic learned through repeated play and failures
Escalated tension through series progression requiring sustained performance
High-pressure distilled scenario for specific skill training

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Start of periods and after goals
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Environmental feedback creating team-specific rhythm and player engagement

typical_relief_triggers: Scoring a goal (celebrated with team organ song)
typical_tension_triggers: Close game state in final period
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast and frantic pace of skating and slap-shotting; tension escalates through playoff progression from single games to best-of-seven series structure

difficulty_curve.early_game: Exhibition and demo modes establish baseline skill with low stakes; player learns core mechanics (skating, passing, shooting) against variable AI
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progression to single-game playoff modes introduces match-based pressure; one-timer and manual goalie control become strategically important
difficulty_curve.late_game: Best-of-seven playoff series demands consistency across multiple games; shootout mode provides high-pressure distilled challenge
notable_spikes: Transition from exhibition to playoffs (stakes elevation)
Best-of-seven series vs. single-game playoffs (stamina and psychological pressure)
Shootout mini-game (sudden-death tension, isolated skill test)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game modes function as difficulty steps: Demo → Exhibition Game → Single Game Playoffs → Best of Seven Playoffs → Shootout. Each mode escalates stakes and required consistency while maintaining identical core mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Exhibition Game (safe mastery) to Best of Seven Playoffs (sustained pressure)

notable_level_patterns.description: Team-specific organ songs mark period transitions and goals, creating recognizable cadence that reinforces team identity and provides psychological relief/celebration markers within matches
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Hartford Whalers' 'Brass Bonanza' signals structural moments and scoring events

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-game playoffs offer quick high-stakes matches; best-of-seven format requires player to maintain performance across multiple games, creating psychological fatigue and tension accumulation
